entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The carbon stored in soil, acting as a nutrient source and maintaining soil integrity

entities.name: CARBON SEQUESTRATION
entities.description: The process of removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it in the soil

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The climatic region where the study was conducted

entities.name: BLACK SOILS
entities.description: A type of soil characterized by specific chemical and physical properties found in the study area

entities.name: RED SOILS
entities.description: A type of soil characterized by specific chemical and physical properties found in the study area

entities.name: HORTICULTURAL SYSTEMS
entities.description: Land use systems focused on garden crops

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS
entities.description: Land use systems focused on crop production

entities.name: PADDY SYSTEMS
entities.description: Land use systems specifically for rice cultivation

entities.name: CLAY FRACTION
entities.description: The proportion of clay particles in the soil

entities.name: TOTAL NITROGEN
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen present in the soil

entities.name: TOTAL PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: The total amount of phosphorus present in the soil

entities.name: PERENNIALS
entities.description: Plants that live for more than two years

entities.name: ANNUAL CROPS
entities.description: Plants that complete their life cycle in one growing season

entities.name: C:N RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of carbon to nitrogen in the soil

entities.name: C:P RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of carbon to phosphorus in the soil

entities.name: SEMI-ARID MOIST ZONE
entities.description: A specific bioclimatic zone within the semi-arid tropics

entities.name: SUB-HUMID MOIST ZONE
entities.description: A specific bioclimatic zone within the semi-arid tropics

entities.name: ARID ZONE
entities.description: A specific bioclimatic zone characterized by very low rainfall

entities.name: SOIL C:N INDEX
entities.description: A calculated index used to evaluate soil fertility and carbon sequestration potential

entities.name: MEAN ANNUAL RAINFALL
entities.description: The average amount of rainfall per year in a given area

entities.name: FOREST SYSTEMS
entities.description: Land use systems dominated by forest cover

entities.name: PERMANENT FALLOW LAND
entities.description: Land left unseeded for a long period

entities.name: CEREAL BASED CROPPING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems focused on cereal crops

entities.name: COTTON
entities.description: A fiber crop used in agricultural systems

entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: A legume crop used in agricultural systems

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the benchmark spots are located

relationships.description: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON is the primary component stored during CARBON SEQUESTRATION
The SEMI-ARID TROPICS are located within INDIA
BLACK SOILS are found in the SEMI-ARID TROPICS
RED SOILS are found in the SEMI-ARID TROPICS
PADDY SYSTEMS have maximum content of SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
HORTICULTURAL SYSTEMS have high SOIL ORGANIC CARBON content
AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS have high SOIL ORGANIC CARBON content
CLAY FRACTION influences the TOTAL NITROGEN content
CLAY FRACTION influences the TOTAL PHOSPHORUS content
CLAY FRACTION influences SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
SOIL ORGANIC CARBON shows significant positive correlation with TOTAL NITROGEN
SOIL ORGANIC CARBON shows significant positive correlation with TOTAL PHOSPHORUS
PERENNIALS sequester carbon better than ANNUAL CROPS
ANNUAL CROPS sequester carbon less effectively than PERENNIALS
C:N RATIO is computed using SOIL ORGANIC CARBON to identify soil quality
C:P RATIO is computed using SOIL ORGANIC CARBON to identify soil quality
C:N RATIO was highest under SEMI-ARID MOIST ZONE in BLACK SOILS
C:P RATIO was highest under SUB-HUMID MOIST ZONE
SOIL C:N INDEX is developed using SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
SOIL C:N INDEX is developed using TOTAL NITROGEN stocks
RED SOILS showed higher fertility in terms of SOIL ORGANIC CARBON compared to BLACK SOILS
SEMI-ARID MOIST ZONE in BLACK SOILS had the highest SOIL C:N INDEX
As MEAN ANNUAL RAINFALL decreased, the SOIL C:N INDEX increased
HORTICULTURAL SYSTEMS had a better SOIL C:N INDEX than AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS in BLACK SOILS
FOREST SYSTEMS had a better SOIL C:N INDEX than AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS
PERMANENT FALLOW LAND has the potential to sequester carbon
CEREAL BASED CROPPING SYSTEMS sequester more carbon than COTTON and SOYBEAN systems
COTTON based systems sequester less carbon than CEREAL BASED CROPPING SYSTEMS
SOYBEAN based systems sequester less carbon than CEREAL BASED CROPPING SYSTEMS
